summary.brief: Bhagwant v. Kent School Corp.. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: The plaintiff, Rajinda Bhagwant, a resident of New York, initiated this action against the defendants, the Kent School Corporation (hereinafter “Kent”), Joel Danisi, M.D., and Marie Savan, alleging medical malpractice and negligence.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At approximately 4:10 p.m. on November 17, 2002, Bhagwant, a student at the school, arrived at the Infirmary complaining of a pain in his right groin, a bump on his right testicle, and a tender right abdomen, upper thigh, and lower thigh.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: During Bhagwant’s visit to the Infirmary at 4:10 p.m., Savan did not examine Bhagwant’s testicle or groin.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Around 7:40 a.m. on the next day, Bhagwant returned to the Infirmary. At 11:00 a.m., Danisi examined Bhagwant and diagnosed him with epididymitis.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Bhagwant underwent surgery at Lincoln Hospital to remove his right testicle on August 19, 2003.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Lynn’s affidavit, also dated January 5, 2006, essentially states that Danisi departed from accepted medical practice on August 26, 2002, the day he signed Kent’s standing orders.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Stephen Lynn

evidence_index.text: Therefore, Savan and Kent’s Motion for Summary Judgment is denied.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: As a result, this court denies Danisi’s Motion for Summary Judgment.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Pain in right groin, bump on right testicle, tender right abdomen, upper thigh, and lower thigh
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Pain began by 12 noon on November 17, 2002
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Groin pain with possible testicular involvement

requestables.name: Initial Infirmary Visit
requestables.description: Documentation of initial visit to infirmary

reveal.result_summary: Patient presented with groin pain, bump on testicle, and tenderness
reveal.clinical_significance: Potential testicular torsion not ruled out
reveal.decision_impact: Should have prompted immediate examination and possible referral

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to infirmary with acute symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in infirmary with groin pain and testicular bump
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Perform immediate physical examination of the groin and testicle
options.clinical_reasoning: Essential to rule out testicular torsion

options.description: Provide ibuprofen and discharge with instructions to return next day
options.clinical_reasoning: Delayed examination could miss critical diagnosis

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant provided ibuprofen and discharged patient

expected_action_court.description: Immediate examination should have been performed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated examination was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to examine led to missed diagnosis of testicular torsion

patient_outcome.description: Patient underwent surgery to remove right testicle
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Loss of testicle, potential fertility issues

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to examine and diagnose testicular torsion
Causation established - earlier diagnosis would have prevented loss of testicle

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to examine and diagnose testicular torsion
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Immediate examination could have led to timely intervention

allegations.allegation: Failure to examine and diagnose testicular torsion
